children.text: Greeting & Empathy
Dear Confused Mum,
children.cn: 亲爱的困惑妈妈，

children.text: I understand how you feel.
children.cn: 我理解您的感受。

children.text: You are worried because your daughter does not ask for your advice now.
children.cn: 您感到担忧，因为您的女儿现在不向您寻求建议了。
问候与共情

children.text: Analysis & Advice
This behaviour is normal for teenagers.
children.cn: 这种行为对青少年来说是正常的。

children.text: She wants to grow up and be responsible for herself.
children.cn: 她想要长大，并对自己负责。

children.text: I recommend that you should give her some space.
children.cn: 我建议您应该给她一些空间。

children.text: You can talk to her like a friend.
children.cn: 您可以像朋友一样和她谈谈。
分析与建议

children.text: Closing
I hope my advice will help you.
children.cn: 希望我的建议能帮到您。

children.text: Best wishes,
children.cn: 最美好的祝愿，

children.text: Susan Luo
children.cn: 苏珊·罗
结语

tree.cn: 给困惑妈妈的回信（基础版）

children.text: Greeting & Empathy
Dear Confused Mum,
children.cn: 亲爱的困惑妈妈，

children.text: I understand quite well that you are anxious and feel terrible about your daughter's recent changes.
children.highlight: understand quite well that, feel terrible about
children.cn: 我非常理解您对女儿最近的变化感到焦虑和难受。

children.text: It is hard when teenagers of her generation start to make decisions on their own.
children.highlight: It is hard when, of her generation, on their own
children.cn: 当她这一代的青少年开始独立做决定时，这确实很令人难受。
问候与共情

children.text: Analysis & Advice
However, please realise that wanting independence is a natural part of growing up.
children.highlight: However, realise that, natural part of
children.cn: 然而，请意识到渴望独立是成长过程中很自然的一部分。

children.text: She is simply learning to be responsible for her own choices, such as selecting school courses.
children.highlight: simply learning to, be responsible for, such as
children.cn: 她仅仅是在学习为自己的选择负责，比如选择学校课程。

children.text: Therefore, I recommend that you should listen to her patiently and respect her opinions.
children.highlight: Therefore, recommend that, should, listen to
children.cn: 因此，我建议您应该耐心地倾听她，尊重她的意见。

children.text: Why not discuss problems together as equal friends rather than helicopter parents?
children.highlight: Why not, rather than, helicopter parents
children.cn: 为什么不作为平等的朋友一起讨论问题，而不是当一个直升机父母呢？
分析与建议

children.text: Closing & Encouragement
I am confident that she will value your support once she feels respected.
children.highlight: am confident that, value your support, once
children.cn: 我相信，一旦她感到被尊重，她就会珍视您的支持。

children.text: All the best,
children.cn: 祝一切顺利，

children.text: Susan Luo
children.cn: 苏珊·罗
结语与鼓励

tree.cn: 给困惑妈妈的回信（高级版）
